sourceMovie.title: Wreck-It Ralph
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wreck-It Ralph for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Rich Moore keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The family and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Toy Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wreck-It Ralph clicked for you, Toy Story (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wreck-It Ralph?
faq.answer: Try Toy Story, Cars, Minions—each shares DNA with Wreck-It Ralph while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wreck-It Ralph?
faq.answer: Wreck-It Ralph (2012) is best described as Family, Animation, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wreck-It Ralph part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wreck-It Ralph Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
